babyDevelopment: The heart begins to form and starts beating! This is a major milestone. The neural tube, which will become the brain and spinal cord, is closing. Major organs like the liver and kidneys are starting their initial development.
bodyChanges: Morning sickness often kicks in now. You might feel very tired and need to urinate more frequently. Your breasts may feel heavier and more sensitive as they prepare for future nursing.
tips: Keep crackers by your bedside for morning nausea
Avoid skipping meals; an empty stomach worsens nausea
Check all supplements for pregnancy safety

do: Drink at least 2.5L of water daily

travelAdvisory.note: Safe, but pack snacks for nausea management.

warningSignsToWatch: Inability to keep any fluids down (Hyperemesis)
milestoneDescription: Your baby's heart is beating for the first time.
